issues.title: Object URL not revoked when file is replaced (memory leak)
issues.description: In `handleFileChange`, when the user selects a new file while a previous preview URL already exists, `URL.createObjectURL(file)` is called without first revoking the previous blob URL. This leaks memory each time the user changes the selected file. The sibling component `ReceiptUpload.tsx` correctly handles this by using a functional setState that revokes the old URL before creating a new one.
issues.impact: Each time a user swaps the receipt file, the previous blob URL is orphaned in browser memory and never garbage-collected until page navigation. On long-lived sessions this may accumulate.

issues.title: Unsafe `as number` cast on possibly-undefined Map.get result
issues.description: `insertOrderItems` uses `priceMap.get(item.id) as number` without a prior existence check. While the calling code at line 165-178 validates that each item's price exists before reaching `insertOrderItems`, this creates a fragile coupling: `insertOrderItems` is a separate function whose contract does not express the precondition, and any future caller that bypasses the validation will silently insert `undefined` as a price, resulting in `NaN` or a database type error at runtime. The same pattern exists in `apps/payments/src/features/checkout/infrastructure/checkoutQueries.ts` line 156.
issues.impact: If `priceMap` does not contain a product ID (e.g. due to a refactor adding a new call site without the validation guard), `unit_price_cop` will be `undefined`, which will either insert a NULL into the database or corrupt the order total.

issues.title: Auto-save triggers on initial mount, sending unnecessary API requests
issues.description: The three `useEffect` hooks for display_blocks and form_fields (lines 69-78) run on first mount as well as on every subsequent change. This means every time the `PaymentMethodEditor` mounts, it immediately sends up to 3 PATCH requests to the server with the initial (unchanged) values. The name effect has a guard (`if (!nameEn.trim()) return`) but the other two have none.
issues.impact: Unnecessary network requests on every editor mount. This could trigger rate limiting, cause false 'saving' indicators, or overwrite any concurrent server-side changes with stale initial form values.

issues.title: ProductFilters sync effect creates a URL update loop risk
issues.description: Two `useEffect` hooks create a dependency cycle: the first (line 26) debounces `searchInput` → URL param `q`, and the second (line 34) syncs URL param `q` → `searchInput`. If `q` in the URL changes (e.g. via browser back/forward), it sets `searchInput`, which then triggers the debounce effect, which sets `q` in the URL again, which fires the sync effect again. In practice this may be benign because `nuqs` uses `{ history: 'replace' }` and only updates if the value actually changes, but the circular pattern is fragile. The same pattern exists in `apps/admin/src/features/users/presentation/pages/UsersPageContent.tsx`.
issues.impact: Potential extra URL writes on browser back/forward navigation. Low risk in practice due to nuqs deduplication, but could cause subtle issues if the URL normalization behavior changes.

patterns.pattern: Blob URL management via ref+effect pattern
patterns.description: Two components (ReceiptUpload, ResubmitEvidenceForm) use a ref+useEffect to track and revoke blob URLs on unmount. ReceiptUpload correctly revokes on replacement; ResubmitEvidenceForm does not. Consider extracting a shared useObjectUrl hook.
patterns.recommendation: Extract blob URL lifecycle into a reusable hook to enforce consistent revocation on both replacement and unmount.

patterns.description: Multiple components (ProductFilters in studio and store, UsersPageContent in admin) use the same two-effect pattern: input→debounce→URL and URL→input-sync. This is a well-established pattern in the codebase but contains latent cycle risk.
patterns.recommendation: Document the pattern in a shared hook or comment, and always guard the sync-back effect with an equality check.
